idea.hook: What if you tried to land on a Neutron Star? 🤯
facts: It's the crushed core of a massive star, only ~12 miles (20 km) wide!
Surface gravity is 2 BILLION times Earth's. You'd be instantly squashed flatter than paper.
It spins up to 700 times per second, creating immense centrifugal force.
Magnetic field is 1 TRILLION times stronger than Earth's, ripping atoms apart.
idea.payoff: You wouldn't "land." You'd be vaporized into a plasma long before impact!
idea.title: Land on a Neutron Star?! 😱💫

scenes.text: What if you tried to land on a Neutron Star? 🤯
scenes.visual: A distant, ominous neutron star in the deep dark void of space, surrounded by a faint, eerie glow.

scenes.text: Only ~12 miles (20 km) wide! The crushed core of a massive star.
scenes.visual: A neutron star zooms closer, its surface barely visible. An outline of a small city (e.g., Manhattan) appears next to it for scale comparison.

scenes.text: Gravity: 2 BILLION x Earth's! Instantly squashed flatter than paper.
scenes.visual: An abstract visual of immense crushing force. A human-like silhouette approaches the star, then instantly flattens into a 2D disc, perhaps with a red-shift distortion.

scenes.text: Spins 700x/sec! Mag field 1 TRILLION x Earth's, ripping atoms apart.
scenes.visual: A rapidly spinning neutron star visual, with highly energetic, arcing magnetic field lines visible. Plasma surges and flickers around its poles.

scenes.text: You wouldn't 'land.' You'd be vaporized into plasma long before impact!
scenes.visual: A brilliant, blinding flash of light followed by an expanding cloud of superheated plasma. The neutron star briefly disappears behind the explosion, then reappears, pulsating intensely.
